IterableElementBase. Base class that makes a data structure iterable and provides common
element-wise utilities (e.g., map/filter/reduce/find).
IterableElementBase.[Symbol.iterator](args: unknown[]). returns IterableIterator<E>. Returns an iterator over the structure's elements. Time complexity: O(n). Tags: lookup, read-only, iteration
IterableElementBase.values(). returns IterableIterator<E>. Returns an iterator over the values (alias of the default iterator). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: iteration

IterableElementBase.every(predicate: ElementCallback<E, R, boolean>, thisArg: unknown). returns boolean. Tests whether all elements satisfy the predicate. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)

IterableElementBase.some(predicate: ElementCallback<E, R, boolean>, thisArg: unknown). returns boolean. Tests whether at least one element satisfies the predicate. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)

IterableElementBase.forEach(callbackfn: ElementCallback<E, R, void>, thisArg: unknown). returns void. Invokes a callback for each element in iteration order. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: iteration
IterableElementBase.find(predicate: ElementCallback<E, R, boolean>, thisArg: unknown). returns E | undefined. . Tags: lookup, read-only
IterableElementBase.has(element: E). returns boolean. Checks whether a strictly-equal element exists in the structure. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: predicate, read-only

IterableElementBase.reduce(callbackfn: ReduceElementCallback<E, R, U>, initialValue: U | undefined). returns U. Reduces all elements to a single accumulated value. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: functional, transformation
IterableElementBase.toArray(). returns E[]. Materializes the elements into a new array. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)

IterableElementBase.toVisual(). returns E[]. Returns a representation of the structure suitable for quick visualization.
Defaults to an array of elements; subclasses may override to provide richer visuals. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
IterableElementBase.print(). returns void. Prints `toVisual()` to the console. Intended for quick debugging. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
IterableElementBase.isEmpty(). returns boolean. Indicates whether the structure currently contains no elements. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: predicate, read-only
IterableElementBase.clear(). returns void. Removes all elements from the structure. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: bulk-delete, mutating
IterableElementBase.clone(). returns this. Creates a structural copy with the same element values and configuration. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: copying, read-only

IterableElementBase.map(callback: ElementCallback<E, R, EM>, options: IterableElementBaseOptions<EM, RM> | undefined, thisArg: unknown). returns IterableElementBase<EM, RM>. Maps each element to a new element and returns a new iterable structure. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: functional, transformation

IterableElementBase.mapSame(callback: ElementCallback<E, R, E>, thisArg: unknown). returns this. Maps each element to the same element type and returns the same concrete structure type. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: functional, transformation

IterableElementBase.filter(predicate: ElementCallback<E, R, boolean>, thisArg: unknown). returns this. Filters elements using the provided predicate and returns the same concrete structure type. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(k). Tags: functional, transformation
IterableEntryBase. Iterable view over key-value entries.

IterableEntryBase.[Symbol.iterator](args: any[]). returns IterableIterator<[K, V]>. Default iterator yielding `[key, value]` entries. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: lookup, read-only, iteration
IterableEntryBase.entries(). returns IterableIterator<[K, V | undefined]>. Iterate over `[key, value]` pairs (may yield `undefined` values). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)
IterableEntryBase.keys(). returns IterableIterator<K>. Iterate over keys only. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)
IterableEntryBase.values(). returns IterableIterator<V>. Iterate over values only. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: iteration
IterableEntryBase.every(predicate: EntryCallback<K, V, boolean>, thisArg: any). returns boolean. Test whether all entries satisfy the predicate. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)
IterableEntryBase.some(predicate: EntryCallback<K, V, boolean>, thisArg: any). returns boolean. Test whether any entry satisfies the predicate. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)
IterableEntryBase.forEach(callbackfn: EntryCallback<K, V, void>, thisArg: any). returns void. Visit each entry, left-to-right. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: iteration

IterableEntryBase.find(callbackfn: EntryCallback<K, V, boolean>, thisArg: any). returns [K, V] | undefined. Find the first entry that matches a predicate. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: lookup, read-only
IterableEntryBase.has(key: K). returns boolean. Whether the given key exists. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: predicate, read-only
IterableEntryBase.hasValue(value: V). returns boolean. Whether there exists an entry with the given value. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: predicate, read-only
IterableEntryBase.get(key: K). returns V | undefined. Get the value under a key. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: lookup, read-only

IterableEntryBase.reduce(callbackfn: ReduceEntryCallback<K, V, U>, initialValue: U). returns U. Reduce entries into a single accumulator. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: functional, transformation
IterableEntryBase.toArray(). returns [K, V][]. Converts data structure to `[key, value]` pairs. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
IterableEntryBase.toVisual(). returns string | [K, V][]. Visualize the iterable as an array of `[key, value]` pairs (or a custom string). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
IterableEntryBase.print(). returns void. Print a human-friendly representation to the console. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
IterableEntryBase.isEmpty(). returns boolean. Whether there are no entries. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: predicate, read-only
IterableEntryBase.clear(). returns void. Remove all entries. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: bulk-delete, mutating
IterableEntryBase.clone(). returns this. Deep clone preserving the concrete subtype. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: copying, read-only
IterableEntryBase.map(args: any[]). returns any. Map entries using an implementation-specific strategy. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: functional, transformation
IterableEntryBase.filter(args: any[]). returns this. Filter entries and return the same-species structure. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: functional, transformation
LinkedListNode. Singly-linked list node. Tags: node-based, pointer-based
LinearBase. Abstract linear container with array-like utilities.
LinearBase.indexOf(searchElement: E, fromIndex: number). returns number. First index of a value from the left. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)
LinearBase.lastIndexOf(searchElement: E, fromIndex: number). returns number. Last index of a value from the right. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)

LinearBase.findIndex(predicate: ElementCallback<E, R, boolean>, thisArg: any). returns number. Find the first index matching a predicate. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: lookup, read-only
LinearBase.concat(items: (E | this)[]). returns this. Concatenate elements and/or containers. Time complexity: O(sum(length). Space complexity: O(sum(length). Tags: lookup, read-only
LinearBase.sort(compareFn: ((a: E, b: E) => number) | undefined). returns this. In-place stable order via array sort semantics. Time complexity: O(n log n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: sorting

LinearBase.splice(start: number, deleteCount: number, items: E[]). returns this. Remove and/or insert elements at a position (array-compatible). Time complexity: O(n + m). Space complexity: O(min(n, m)
LinearBase.join(separator: string). returns string. Join all elements into a string. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
LinearBase.toReversedArray(). returns E[]. Snapshot elements into a reversed array. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: reordering, mutating
LinearBase.reduceRight(callbackfn: ReduceLinearCallback<E, U>, initialValue: U | undefined). returns U. . Tags: functional, transformation
LinearBase.slice(start: number, end: number). returns this. Create a shallow copy of a subrange. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n)
LinearBase.fill(value: E, start: number, end: number). returns this. Fill a range with a value. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1)
LinearBase.setAt(index: number, value: E). returns boolean. Set the value at an index. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: insertion, mutating, lookup, read-only
LinearBase.clone(). returns this. Deep clone while preserving concrete subtype. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: copying, read-only
LinearBase.reverse(). returns this. Reverse the order of elements in-place (or equivalent). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: reordering, mutating
LinearBase.push(elementOrNode: E | NODE). returns boolean. Append one element or node to the tail. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: insertion, mutating

LinearBase.pushMany(elements: Iterable<E> | Iterable<R> | Iterable<NODE>). returns boolean[]. Append many elements/nodes at once. Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: insertion, mutating
LinearBase.delete(elementOrNode: E | NODE | undefined). returns boolean. Remove one element or node if present. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: deletion, mutating
LinearBase.at(index: number). returns E | undefined. Get element at an index. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: lookup, read-only
LinearBase.deleteAt(pos: number). returns E | undefined. Remove element at a position. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: deletion, mutating, lookup, read-only

LinearBase.addAt(index: number, newElementOrNode: E | NODE). returns boolean. Insert an element/node at a position. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: insertion, mutating, lookup, read-only
LinearLinkedBase. Linked-list specialized linear container. Tags: node-based, pointer-based

LinearLinkedBase.indexOf(searchElement: E, fromIndex: number). returns number. Linked-list optimized `indexOf` (forwards scan). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: node-based, pointer-based

LinearLinkedBase.lastIndexOf(searchElement: E, fromIndex: number). returns number. Linked-list optimized `lastIndexOf` (reverse scan). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: node-based, pointer-based

LinearLinkedBase.concat(items: (E | LinearBase<E, R, LinkedListNode<E>>)[]). returns this. Concatenate lists/elements preserving order. Time complexity: O(sum(length). Space complexity: O(sum(length). Tags: node-based, pointer-based, lookup, read-only

LinearLinkedBase.slice(start: number, end: number). returns this. Slice via forward iteration (no random access required). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(n). Tags: node-based, pointer-based

LinearLinkedBase.splice(start: number, deleteCount: number, items: E[]). returns this. Splice by walking node iterators from the start index. Time complexity: O(n + m). Space complexity: O(min(n, m). Tags: node-based, pointer-based
LinearLinkedBase.reduceRight(callbackfn: ReduceLinearCallback<E, U>, initialValue: U | undefined). returns U. . Tags: node-based, pointer-based, functional, transformation

LinearLinkedBase.delete(elementOrNode: E | NODE | undefined). returns boolean. Delete by element or node in a linked list. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: node-based, pointer-based, deletion, mutating

LinearLinkedBase.addBefore(existingElementOrNode: E | NODE, newElementOrNode: E | NODE). returns boolean. Insert new element/node before an existing node. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: node-based, pointer-based, insertion, mutating

LinearLinkedBase.addAfter(existingElementOrNode: E | NODE, newElementOrNode: E | NODE). returns boolean. Insert new element/node after an existing node. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: node-based, pointer-based, insertion, mutating

LinearLinkedBase.getNodeAt(index: number). returns NODE | undefined. Node at index (for random-access emulation). Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: node-based, pointer-based, lookup, read-only

AVLTreeNode. Represents a Node in an AVL (Adelson-Velsky and Landis) Tree.
It extends a BSTNode and ensures the 'height' property is maintained. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree. Represents a self-balancing AVL (Adelson-Velsky and Landis) Tree.
This tree extends BST and performs rotations on set/delete to maintain balance. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree.createNode(key: K, value: V | undefined). returns AVLTreeNode<K, V>. (Protected) Creates a new AVL tree node. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree.isNode(keyNodeOrEntry: K | AVLTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is an `AVLTreeNode` instance. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree.set(keyNodeOrEntry: K | AVLTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, value: V | undefined). returns boolean. Sets a new node to the AVL tree and balances the tree path. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree.delete(keyNodeOrEntry: K | AVLTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns BinaryTreeDeleteResult<AVLTreeNode<K, V>>[]. Deletes a node from the AVL tree and re-balances the tree. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree.perfectlyBalance(iterationType: IterationType). returns boolean. Rebuilds the tree to be perfectly balanced. Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(N). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

AVLTree.map(callback: EntryCallback<K, V | undefined, [MK, MV]>, options: Partial<BinaryTreeOptions<MK, MV, MR>> | undefined, thisArg: unknown). returns AVLTree<MK, MV, MR>. Creates a new AVLTree by mapping each [key, value] pair. Time complexity: O(N log N). Space complexity: O(N). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
BinaryIndexedTree. . Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
BinaryIndexedTree.readSingle(index: number). returns number. The function "readSingle" reads a single number from a specified index. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryIndexedTree.update(position: number, change: number). returns void. The "update" function updates the value at a given index by adding a delta and triggers a callback
to notify of the change. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryIndexedTree.writeSingle(index: number, freq: number). returns void. The function "writeSingle" checks the index and writes a single value with a given frequency. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryIndexedTree.read(count: number). returns number. The read function takes a count parameter, checks if it is an integer, and returns the result of
calling the _read function with the count parameter clamped between 0 and the maximum value. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryIndexedTree.lowerBound(sum: number). returns number. The function returns the lower bound of a non-descending sequence that sums up to a given number. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryIndexedTree.upperBound(sum: number). returns number. The upperBound function returns the index of the first element in a sequence that is greater than
or equal to a given sum. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryIndexedTree.getPrefixSum(i: number). returns number. The function calculates the prefix sum of an array using a binary indexed tree. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
BinaryTreeNode. . Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
BinaryTree. A general Binary Tree implementation. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.createNode(key: K, value: V | undefined). returns BinaryTreeNode<K, V>. (Protected) Creates a new node. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.createTree(options: Partial<BinaryTreeOptions<K, V, R>> | undefined). returns this. Creates a new, empty tree of the same type and configuration. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.ensureNode(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null | undefined. Ensures the input is a node. If it's a key or entry, it searches for the node. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isNode(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is a `BinaryTreeNode` instance. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isRaw(keyNodeEntryOrRaw: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | R | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is a raw data object (R) that needs conversion via `toEntryFn`. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isRealNode(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is a "real" node (i.e., not null, undefined, or NIL). Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isRealNodeOrNull(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is either a "real" node or null. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isNIL(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is the sentinel NIL node. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isRange(keyNodeEntryOrPredicate: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | NodePredicate<BinaryTreeNode<K, V>> | Range<K> | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is a `Range` object. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isLeaf(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if a node is a leaf (has no real children). Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isEntry(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the given item is a [key, value] entry pair. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isValidKey(key: any). returns boolean. Checks if the given key is valid (comparable or null). Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.add(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Adds a new node to the tree. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(N). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.set(keyNodeOrEntry: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, value: V | undefined). returns boolean. Adds or updates a new node to the tree. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(N). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.addMany(keysNodesEntriesOrRaws: Iterable<K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | R | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined>). returns boolean[]. Adds multiple items to the tree. Time complexity: O(N * M). Space complexity: O(M). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.setMany(keysNodesEntriesOrRaws: Iterable<K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | R | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined>, values: Iterable<V | undefined> | undefined). returns boolean[]. Adds or updates multiple items to the tree. Time complexity: O(N * M). Space complexity: O(M). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.merge(anotherTree: BinaryTree<K, V, R>). returns void. Merges another tree into this one by seting all its nodes. Time complexity: O(N * M). Space complexity: O(M). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.refill(keysNodesEntriesOrRaws: Iterable<K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | R | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined>, values: Iterable<V | undefined> | undefined). returns void. Clears the tree and refills it with new items. Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(M). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.delete(keyNodeEntryRawOrPredicate: BTNRep<K, V, BinaryTreeNode<K, V>> | NodePredicate<BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null>). returns BinaryTreeDeleteResult<BinaryTreeNode<K, V>>[]. Deletes a node from the tree. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.search(keyNodeEntryOrPredicate: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | NodePredicate<BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null> | null | undefined, onlyOne: boolean, callback: C, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns ReturnType<C>[]. Searches the tree for nodes matching a predicate. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getNodes(keyNodeEntryOrPredicate: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | NodePredicate<BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null> | null | undefined, onlyOne: boolean, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns (BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null)[]. . Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getNode(keyNodeEntryOrPredicate: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | NodePredicate<BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null> | null | undefined, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null | undefined. Gets the first node matching a predicate. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.get(keyNodeEntryOrPredicate: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns V | undefined. Gets the value associated with a key. Time complexity: O(log N). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.has(keyNodeEntryOrPredicate: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | NodePredicate<BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null> | null | undefined, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns boolean. . Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
BinaryTree.clear(). returns void. Clears the tree of all nodes and values. Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
BinaryTree.isEmpty(). returns boolean. Checks if the tree is empty. Time complexity: O(1). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isPerfectlyBalanced(startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns boolean. Checks if the tree is perfectly balanced. Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.isBST(startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns boolean. Checks if the tree is a valid Binary Search Tree (BST). Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getDepth(dist: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined). returns number. Gets the depth of a node (distance from `startNode`). Time complexity: O(H). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getHeight(startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns number. Gets the maximum height of the tree (longest path from startNode to a leaf). Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getMinHeight(startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns number. Gets the minimum height of the tree (shortest path from startNode to a leaf). Time complexity: O(N). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getPathToRoot(beginNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, callback: C, isReverse: boolean). returns ReturnType<C>[]. Gets the path from a given node up to the root. Time complexity: O(H). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getLeftMost(callback: C, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns ReturnType<C>. Finds the leftmost node in a subtree (the node with the smallest key in a BST). Time complexity: O(H). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getRightMost(callback: C, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType). returns ReturnType<C>. Finds the rightmost node in a subtree (the node with the largest key in a BST). Time complexity: O(H). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getPredecessor(node: BinaryTreeNode<K, V>). returns BinaryTreeNode<K, V>. Gets the Morris traversal predecessor (rightmost node in the left subtree, or node itself). Time complexity: O(H). Space complexity: O(1). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.getSuccessor(x: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null | undefined). returns BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | null | undefined. Gets the in-order successor of a node in a BST. Time complexity: O(H). Space complexity: O(H). Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

BinaryTree.dfs(callback: C, pattern: DFSOrderPattern, onlyOne: boolean, startNode: K | BinaryTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined, iterationType: IterationType, includeNull: boolean). returns ReturnType<C>[]. close to the expected insertion position (often the previously returned node in a loop).

When the hint is a good fit (sorted / nearly-sorted insertion), this can avoid most of the
normal root-to-leaf search and reduce constant factors.

TreeMultiMap. TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/..`) return entry tuples like TreeMap. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

TreeSet. An ordered Set backed by a red-black tree.

- Iteration order is ascending by key.
- No node exposure: all APIs use keys only. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

Supports:
- `number` (rejects `NaN`; treats `-0` and `0` as equal)
- `string`
- `Date` (orders by `getTime()`, rejects invalid dates)

For other key types, a custom comparator must be provided. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access
